title: Carica lanceolata
taxonomy: Carica lanceolata is a species within the genus Carica, which is a part of the family Caricaceae. This family consists of flowering plants commonly known as papaya relatives. Caricaceae is a part of the order Cucurbitales, a large group of flowering plants that also includes cucumbers, melons, and squash. The Cucurbitales order is part of the clade Rosidae, which includes a wide variety of flowering plants. This classification places Carica lanceolata within the larger group of flowering plants that are characterized by their fruit-bearing structures.

In some regions, Carica lanceolata is used to treat digestive problems and skin conditions. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal potential and its effects on human health.
